_comment: THE THOUSAND-KNOT BAZAAR — a sprawling fantasy market wound around the dry bed of the Sleeping River (the river that 'was sold'). Reached from the Vale through the sun-warmed archway. PILLARS: (1) NAVIGATION — the Vein (a sunken dry channel) runs the world's width and every Knot's paths drain into it; five tall VANES are the skyline compass; two KNUCKLE plazas anchor the middle. (2) ATMOSPHERE & LORE — five KNOT districts, each a tinted region with its own palette, Vane, and one story fragment (Examine its lore signpost or a Knuckle notice-board). Stalls are placed procedurally (see tools/gen_bazaar.py) so rows feel packed but stay walkable — tidy in Spice (onboarding), tangled in the Ragpicker's. +y is south, -y is north; colors [r,g,b] 0..1. world_art draws by 'type'; new types: vein + chimney/prism_tower/ivory_spire/crooked_mast/sky_anchor (Vanes), dry_well/notice_board/shrine, wanderer, crate. A prop's optional 'lore' is read out on Examine.
_props_comment: All the props world_art draws, grouped in array order: the five Vanes — the skyline compass, one tall landmark per Knot; the Knuckle plazas: dry well, notice-board, rest shrine; one lore signpost per Knot (Examine to read its story fragment); the wet-boots man (Ragpicker's Tangle quest seed); the colour merchant + shop (Spice Knot onboarding); Spice Knot stalls (procedurally packed; tidy rows — onboarding); Glassblowers' Run stalls; Bonewrights' Knot stalls (quieter, paler); Ragpicker's Tangle stalls + crates (densest, maziest); High Stalls (wealthiest, gilded) + fine cargo; lanterns. A prop is static scenery unless it opts in with "interactive": true (the Vanes, Knuckle furniture, lore signposts, the wet-boots man, and the merchant — each does something or reads out a lore line on Examine; stalls/crates/lanterns just convey themselves by appearance). Stalls/crates are generated by tools/gen_bazaar.py — edit that and re-run to re-pack, don't hand-tune positions.

props.label: the Copper Chimney
props.lore: The Copper Chimney, forever smoking over the Spice Knot. Lost? Find its smoke and you've found your way.

props.label: the Prism Tower
props.lore: The Prism Tower throws rainbows over the Glassblowers' Run — a colour you can steer by from half the bazaar.

props.label: the Ivory Finger
props.lore: The Ivory Finger, a carved spire pointing skyward over the hushed Bonewrights' Knot.

props.label: the Crooked Mast
props.lore: The Crooked Mast leans over the Ragpicker's Tangle like a ship run aground in a river that left without it.

props.label: the Sky-Anchor
props.lore: The Sky-Anchor hangs over the High Stalls on its chains — visible clear across the channel, for an anchor with no water to drop into.

props.label: the dry fountain
props.lore: A dry fountain. There's a green stain a hand's-width above the floor — the old waterline. The river stood this high, once.

props.label: the South Knuckle notice-board
props.lore: THE THOUSAND-KNOT BAZAAR, in the bed of the Sleeping River. The river didn't dry up — it was sold, in a bargain no one quite remembers. The Knots are the slow repayment of that debt.

props.label: a roadside shrine
props.lore: A little shrine with a steady candle. A good place to stop a while, here at the bazaar's main crossing.

props.label: the dry fountain
props.lore: Another dry fountain, another high-water mark. Some vendors keep them swept, as if expecting the water back any morning now.

props.label: the North Knuckle notice-board
props.lore: All roads run downhill to the Vein — follow the dry channel and you'll never be lost. They say the water returns when the debt is paid. A few quiet vendors are betting on when.

props.label: a roadside shrine
props.lore: A candle gutters in a stone niche. From here the climb north leads up to the High Stalls and the long views.

props.label: a painted signboard
props.lore: The first stalls were boats. Look at the curve of those old roofs — they were hulls, beached in the dry channel and never refloated.

props.label: an etched glass placard
props.lore: We melt sand from the dry bed. Every window in the Run is a piece of the river, made to hold light instead of water.

props.label: a carved bone marker
props.lore: The Bonewrights keep the founder's ledgers. The page with the name is missing. It was removed, not lost.

props.label: a tin scrap-sign
props.lore: Someone down here sells river-charts. Charts of a river that doesn't exist yet. Ask for the man with wet boots.

props.label: a weathered vista-sign
props.lore: From up here you see the whole channel. On still mornings the dust in it moves like a current. The ones who watch are the ones who bet.

props.label: a man with wet boots
props.lore: His boots are wet — in a city built on a dry river, that means something. He deals in charts of water not yet returned, and asks only one thing: when?
